content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array a1,‚Ä¶,ana_1,\ldots,a_na1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,an‚Äč of nnn elements. Initially, all elements of the array are equal to 111. There are two types of operations.



111  iii:  Take the index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§n1 \le i \le n1‚Č§i‚Č§n), and multiply it by 222 (ai:=2‚čÖaia_i := 2 \cdot a_iai‚Äč:=2‚čÖai‚Äč).




222 iii jjj:  Take two indexes iii and jjj (1‚Č§i,j‚Č§n,i‚Č†j1 \le i, j \le n, i \ne j1‚Č§i,j‚Č§n,iÓÄ†=j), and subtract aja_jaj‚Äč from aia_iai‚Äč, (ai:=ai‚ąíaja_i := a_i - a_jai‚Äč:=ai‚Äč‚ąíaj‚Äč).



All elements must be positive after each operation, and at most 10910^9109.

In addition, you are given an array bbb. Chef wants to make bbb from aaa. Help Chef to reach the array bbb in no more than 43 00043\ 00043 000 operations, or determine it is impossible.

###Input:



The first line contains a single integer nnn - size of the array.




The second line contains nnn integers b1,‚Ä¶bnb_1, \ldots b_nb1‚Äč,‚Ä¶bn‚Äč - the array bbb.



###Output:

If it is impossible to reach bbb, print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.

Otherwise, output kkk - the number of operations (0‚Č§k‚Č§43 0000 \le k \le 43\ 0000‚Č§k‚Č§43 000). On the next kkk lines, print the operations as described in the statement.

If there are multiple solutions, you may output any.

###Constraints


1‚Č§n‚Č§1031 \leq n \leq 10^31‚Č§n‚Č§103
1‚Č§bi‚Č§1061 \leq b_i \leq 10^61‚Č§bi‚Äč‚Č§106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Initially, the array aaa is [1,1,1][1,1,1][1,1,1].
By the first operation, a3:=2‚čÖa3a_3:=2\cdot a_3a3‚Äč:=2‚čÖa3‚Äč, so the array becomes [1,1,2][1,1,2][1,1,2].
By the second operation, a3:=2‚čÖa3a_3:=2\cdot a_3a3‚Äč:=2‚čÖa3‚Äč, so the array becomes [1,1,4][1,1,4][1,1,4].
By the third operation, a3:=a3‚ąía1a_3:=a_3-a_1a3‚Äč:=a3‚Äč‚ąía1‚Äč, so the array becomes [1,1,3][1,1,3][1,1,3].
By the fourth operation, a2:=2‚čÖa2a_2:=2\cdot a_2a2‚Äč:=2‚čÖa2‚Äč, so the array becomes [1,2,3][1,2,3][1,2,3]. Now the array is equal to bbb, as desired.
